findings.message: Single-line max-width change confirmed: max-w-[48ch] -> max-w-[72ch]. No other lines modified in this file.

Parent container uses max-w-container and the description's grandparent flex column uses min-w-0 flex-1, so the paragraph is properly flex-constrained. 72ch (~576px at body font) is well under the site container (~1200-1280px).

Mobile overflow risk assessment: max-w-[72ch] is an upper bound, not a fixed width. On a 375px viewport, the arrow buttons (h-12 = 48px each, plus gap-6 = 24px) leave ~255-270px for the flex-1 column. The

collapses to that available width because min-w-0 is set on the grandparent. No horizontal overflow.
findings.recommendation: Manual viewport smoke on 375px mobile recommended for final visual confirmation, but behavior is sound per CSS layout rules.

findings.message: Readability: 72ch is inside the 45-75ch sweet spot. text-balance and leading-relaxed remain appropriate at this width.

Sibling stack
    uses flex flex-wrap items-center justify-center gap-2, independent of the

    's max-width. No visual regression risk to the tags row.

notes:
Width change is a single, well-scoped Tailwind utility swap on the description

(max-w-[48ch] -> max-w-[72ch]). The ch unit is responsive to the paragraph's own font-size, and the parent flex chain (flex min-w-0 flex-1 flex-col inside max-w-container) safely clamps the paragraph on narrow viewports -- no horizontal overflow expected at mobile breakpoints. Readability stays within the 45-75ch sweet spot. text-balance, leading-relaxed, and the sibling stack-tags

    are unaffected. Smoke gates (tsc, eslint, next build) are all green per the orchestrator's pre-QA runs. Data fix: sync endpoint reported updated:4 regenerated:4 missing_en_description:1, and direct Supabase inspection confirmed EN paragraphs no longer carry the leading '-' -- so the production data regression introduced by the stale parser has been fully corrected end-to-end for task-agent, proposal-ai, and doctalk. One repo still has no EN description (missing_en_description:1) but that is a source-README gap, not a parser or carousel bug; locale fallback selectDescription() will gracefully use the PT description. Recommend a quick manual mobile (375px) viewport eyeball after deploy, but no blocking issues.
